hawkish_phrases: Monetary policy will need to continue to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
remaining domestic inflationary pressures are resolving more slowly
Services consumer price inflation has declined to 4.9%
Annual private sector regular average weekly earnings growth...remained elevated at 4.8%
The Budget is provisionally expected to boost CPI inflation by just under ½ of a percentage point at the peak
dovish_phrases: There has been continued progress in disinflation
CPI inflation fell to 1.7% in September
The MPC judges that the labour market continues to loosen
Based on the evolving evidence, a gradual approach to removing policy restraint remains appropriate
most of the remaining persistence in inflation may dissipate quickly as pay and price-setting dynamics continue to normalise
summary: The MPC voted 8-1 to cut Bank Rate by 25bp to 4.75%, with continued disinflation progress noted but one hawkish dissenter citing structural wage and price pressures, and guidance emphasizing that policy must remain restrictive for sufficiently long.
